heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the outside temperature (down 5.3°C) and feels like temperature (down 5.0°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 16%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in light levels (up 15269 lux) and cloud levels (up 1568 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 12911 lux) and cloud levels (up 769 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 1568.0m lower than yesterday, 768.9m lower than the past week, 463.7m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1027m minimum and 5610m maximum, with an average of 1775.3m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 2540m and 2240m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; prepare for cold, chilly weather ahead. Also, dry and less humid conditions and mostly calm with light air movement. Chance of light rain, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and a mix of sun and clouds. Stable conditions are expected under a high pressure system.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
